reasoning: The market price was 0.76 on March 6. Trump's massive 25% tariffs on Canada/Mexico and +10% on China took effect on March 4. While on March 6 he temporarily exempted USMCA-compliant goods from Canada/Mexico until April 2, this only covers about 38% of Canadian and 50% of Mexican imports. The remaining goods from these top trade partners are still subject to 25% duties. Combined with the baseline tariff of ~2.4%, the extra 10% on China, and a 25% global steel/aluminum tariff starting March 12, the underlying math suggests the US weighted average tariff is already on track to exceed 6% in Q2 2025 unless all tariffs are rapidly reversed. The high probability (0.75) reflects the fact that only one quarter needs to average >= 6% to resolve YES, and Trump's active use of aggressive duties already places the effective rate near or above that threshold.
